review_round_1.reviewers: quality-assurance agent (APPROVE w/ 1 WARNING) + separate /code-review workflow (8 finder angles, 38 agents, 23 verified findings) — both INDEPENDENTLY converged on the whole-db clobber race (Root Cause A).

must_fix.A_whole_db_clobber_race: runScanDirectories loadDatabase()s a whole-db snapshot at scan start, holds it across many awaited extractMetadataAndThumbnail calls, then `db.metadata=newMetadata; saveDatabase(db)` clobbers the ENTIRE object. A concurrent POST /api/settings (settings silently revert) or recordServed() lastServedAt write (age signal lost -> a watched transcode can be wrongly aged-out/deleted) made DURING the scan is reverted on disk. Findings server.js:615,743,958 + QA WARNING. Fix dir: before the final save, RE-READ fresh db from disk and write back only scan-owned data (merge newMetadata as metadata) preserving concurrently-written db.settings/folders/folderSettings/progress AND any lastServedAt on surviving entries. Regression test: interleave a settings write / recordServed with a scan, assert neither is lost.
must_fix.B_mount_loss_guard_too_shallow: Guard only checks TOP-LEVEL roots via fs.existsSync. (i) a nested/child mount dropping UNDER a present root -> files non-surviving, rootFolder=present top root, guard doesn't fire, pruneMissing=true prunes them (data loss); (ii) scanDirRecursive swallows a subdir read error (EACCES/EIO ~774) so a transiently-unreadable subtree looks like mass deletion and gets pruned; (iii) selectPrunableIds guard skipped when entry.rootFolder is falsy (legacy pre-backfill entries) -> they prune when root unmounted. Findings server.js:696,553,560,774. Fix dir: make 'could not enumerate this dir/subtree' a first-class scan-incomplete/unreadable signal so entries under ANY un-enumerable path are RETAINED regardless of depth; handle falsy-rootFolder conservatively (derive via matchRootFolder or default retain). Regression tests: nested-mount drop, subdir read error, rootFolder-less legacy entry under a missing root.
must_fix.C_overlap_guard_drops_config_rescan: The new `if(scanState.scanning) return;` makes POST /api/config's fire-and-forget scanDirectories() a silent no-op during a scan -> newly-added folders never indexed (and with interval Off, never recovered). Finding server.js:865. Fix: queue a follow-up rescan ('rescan requested' flag) that runs after the current scan finishes.

should_fix.D_timer_rearm_resets_countdown: POST /api/settings calls armScanTimer() unconditionally; clear+recreate resets the periodic countdown, so saving any setting more often than the interval defers the scan indefinitely. The in-code 'safe no-op when unchanged' comment is WRONG. Finding server.js:963. Fix: re-arm ONLY when scanIntervalMinutes actually changed; fix the comment.
should_fix.E_recordServed_full_db_read_hot_path: recordServed reads+parses the ENTIRE db.json on EVERY /video Range request; the ~10-min throttle skips only the WRITE, not the synchronous full-DB READ on the streaming hot path. Finder F, server.js:380. Fix: keep an in-memory last-persisted-served timestamp per id (or consult in-memory recentlyServed) to short-circuit the throttle WITHOUT a disk read; loadDatabase only when actually persisting.

review_round_1.cleanup: 1) Extract ONE shared 'actively-served protected paths' helper — triplicated at evictTranscodeCache:181-184, sweepAgedTranscodes:269-272, /api/cache/clear:988-991; the two new copies also omit evictTranscodeCache's stale-entry pruning. Keep evictTranscodeCache byte-identical if still frozen, or note if touching it is acceptable now. Also the .tmp.mp4 exclusion predicate is copy-pasted ~4 sites -> one shared filter. 2) Derive the two scan-interval value lists from one source (SCAN_INTERVAL_MINUTE_OPTIONS ~197 vs SCAN_INTERVAL_VALID_VALUES ~903 duplicate the non-zero values). 3) gbToBytes (public/js/common.js): a tiny positive GB input rounds to 0 bytes -> setup.html POSTs cacheMaxBytes:0 -> server 400 with a misleading message; clamp sub-1-byte positives to null (or handle) so the UX message is correct.
review_round_1.non_blocking: sweepAgedTranscodes + evictTranscodeCache each do a full independent readdir+statSync back-to-back at both call sites (double directory pass per produce). Minor; only worth folding in if the shared-enumeration change is low-risk — else tech-debt-track. PE's call.
review_round_1.re_review: After the fix round implements + build-verifies, the coordinator runs a FOCUSED re-review on the changed hunks only (not the full 8-angle sweep unless something looks off). Full suite must stay green; transcode-cache.test.js must stay passing.

env_var_correction: Throughout Discovery/Design/state we used 'FILETUBE_CACHE_CAP' as shorthand for the transcode-cache size-cap env var. That name NEVER existed in code — the real var is TRANSCODE_CACHE_MAX_BYTES (server.js:112, read by effectiveCacheCap). Corrected in all SHIPPING surfaces during T8: README, ARCHITECTURE, ROADMAP, and the T7 UI help text at public/setup.html:191 (which had told users to set the phantom var — user-facing copy, no logic impact, fixed directly by the build-specialist). Grep confirms FILETUBE_CACHE_CAP now appears in ZERO shipping surfaces. Remaining references to it in this state file's earlier history entries / locked_decisions are stale internal shorthand only and are superseded by this note.
description: New 'Automation & Storage' area in Settings (setup.html) plus server-side machinery. Six approved items; DECISIONS LOCKED by Dean (see locked_decisions). CORE: (1) Auto-scan interval — Off/30m/1h/6h/12h/24h, DEFAULT 30m; REPLACES the hardcoded 10-min setInterval at server.js:989 (interval driven by persisted pref); add overlap guard; persist in db.json. (2) Transcode cache age-retention — delete transcoded MP4s not watched in X days; Off/7/14/30/90, default 30; keyed off a LAST-SERVED timestamp we control (db.json), atime as fallback (atime unreliable on noatime/relatime); layers on top of the existing size-based LRU eviction (selectEvictions/parseCacheCap/5GB cap) which stays as the hard backstop. COMPANIONS: (3) Cache size display + 'Clear cache now' button. (4) Expose the transcode size cap (FILETUBE_CACHE_CAP / TRANSCODE_CACHE_MAX_BYTES) in the UI, env var remains override/default source. (5) Last-scanned timestamp + 'Scan now' button, built on /api/scan-status + existing scan path. (6) 'Remove entries for deleted files during scan' preference — DEFAULT ON; prunes db.json entries whose source file no longer exists; MANDATORY mount-loss guard ships regardless of the toggle (a configured root folder entirely missing/unmounted must NOT prune its entries — that's a mount failure, not a deletion). Additive/zero-regression EXCEPT the two intentional behavior changes (10min->30m default; prune made safe/controllable). Ships to prod Docker deantammam/filetube. Two-reviewer QA (quality-assurance agent + separate /code-review pass) required before merge.

locked_decisions.D1_auto_scan: Default 30 minutes. UI options Off/30m/1h/6h/12h/24h. Replaces the hardcoded 10-min setInterval at server.js:989; interval driven by the persisted pref; add overlap guard (no scan while scanState.scanning). Persist server-side in db.json. Intentional behavior change (10min->30m).
locked_decisions.D2_prune_missing: ON by default. Mount-loss guard ships REGARDLESS of the toggle: if a configured root folder is entirely missing/unmounted (server.js:442), do NOT prune any entries under it (mount failure != deletion). Only prune an individual entry when its root IS present but that specific file is gone. Intentional behavior change (unconditional+unsafe prune -> safe+controllable).
locked_decisions.D3_age_retention: Default 30 days, keyed off last-watch. PE risk to design around: atime unreliable on noatime/relatime; PREFER a last-served timestamp we control (db.json), atime as fallback. Layers ON TOP of the size-cap LRU (which stays the hard backstop).
